channel.title: Vox English
channel.description:
Show page: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/
Vox English is a SkepVox series for advanced English learners who want language that sounds precise, idiomatic, and socially aware. Each episode uses an original scene, a nuanced dialogue, a guided explanation, and a lesson guide. Episodes 001-150 target B2-C1, then the show moves into C1-C2 from episode 151. The focus is nuance: grammar choices, collocation, idiom, register, stance, implication, and how English works in real conversation. Lesson guides and transcripts are published at https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/
channel.itunes:author: SkepVox
channel.itunes:summary:
Show page: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/
Vox English is a SkepVox series for advanced English learners who want language that sounds precise, idiomatic, and socially aware. Each episode uses an original scene, a nuanced dialogue, a guided explanation, and a lesson guide. Episodes 001-150 target B2-C1, then the show moves into C1-C2 from episode 151. The focus is nuance: grammar choices, collocation, idiom, register, stance, implication, and how English works in real conversation. Lesson guides and transcripts are published at https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/

item.title: 001 - The Apology Window
item.description:
Lesson guide and full transcript: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/001-the-apology-window
In this episode of Vox English, we work with the present simple and present continuous in advanced professional English.
The episode is called "The Apology Window". Maya and Owen prepare language for a public product-demo statement. The scene turns formal apology language, temporary states, corporate register, and careful negative phrasing into a compact lesson in timing and stance.
This lesson helps you use the present simple and present continuous for stable facts, temporary situations, state and action meanings, developing mental states, and performative verbs.
The lesson guide also explains useful vocabulary and expressions from the episode: apologize, request patience, acknowledge concern, deny regret, contingency, accountability, public statement, product demo, temporary state, and other useful advanced-English phrases.
On the episode page you will find the study guide, comprehension questions, answers, notes on usage, the slow dialogue, the natural-speed dialogue, and the full transcript.
item.content:encoded:
Lesson guide and full transcript: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/001-the-apology-window

In this episode of Vox English, we work with the present simple and present continuous in advanced professional English.

The episode is called "The Apology Window". Maya and Owen prepare language for a public product-demo statement. The scene turns formal apology language, temporary states, corporate register, and careful negative phrasing into a compact lesson in timing and stance.

This lesson helps you use the present simple and present continuous for stable facts, temporary situations, state and action meanings, developing mental states, and performative verbs.

The lesson guide also explains useful vocabulary and expressions from the episode: apologize, request patience, acknowledge concern, deny regret, contingency, accountability, public statement, product demo, temporary state, and other useful advanced-English phrases.

On the episode page you will find the study guide, comprehension questions, answers, notes on usage, the slow dialogue, the natural-speed dialogue, and the full transcript.

item.title: 002 - The Headline Cushion
item.description:
Lesson guide and full transcript: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/002-the-headline-cushion
In this episode of Vox English, we work with headline present simple, reported-news phrases, content-summary present simple, and present continuous forms in advanced workplace English.
The episode is called "The Headline Cushion". Ava and Nolan revise an internal update during a leadership transition. The scene turns headlines, board language, corporate caution, and editorial precision into a lesson in how grammar can shape authority.
This lesson helps you use present simple forms in headlines, reports, summaries, and official updates, and present continuous forms for current action and repeated characteristic behavior.
The lesson guide also explains useful vocabulary and expressions from the episode: internal update, headline, accountability, governance, sourced, leadership transition, legal exposure, interim role, former founder, and other useful advanced-English phrases.
On the episode page you will find the study guide, comprehension questions, answers, notes on usage, the slow dialogue, the natural-speed dialogue, and the full transcript.
item.content:encoded:
Lesson guide and full transcript: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/002-the-headline-cushion

In this episode of Vox English, we work with headline present simple, reported-news phrases, content-summary present simple, and present continuous forms in advanced workplace English.

The episode is called "The Headline Cushion". Ava and Nolan revise an internal update during a leadership transition. The scene turns headlines, board language, corporate caution, and editorial precision into a lesson in how grammar can shape authority.

This lesson helps you use present simple forms in headlines, reports, summaries, and official updates, and present continuous forms for current action and repeated characteristic behavior.

The lesson guide also explains useful vocabulary and expressions from the episode: internal update, headline, accountability, governance, sourced, leadership transition, legal exposure, interim role, former founder, and other useful advanced-English phrases.

On the episode page you will find the study guide, comprehension questions, answers, notes on usage, the slow dialogue, the natural-speed dialogue, and the full transcript.

item.title: 003 - The Embargoed Award
item.description:
Lesson guide and full transcript: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/003-the-embargoed-award
In this episode of Vox English, we work with the past simple and the present perfect in advanced public-communication English.
The episode is called "The Embargoed Award". Priya and Elliot prepare university communication around an award result. The scene turns embargoes, news releases, timing, and professional reputation into a lesson in how English frames completed facts and current relevance.
This lesson helps you use past simple and present perfect with news-report openings, finished time expressions, future time clauses, since clauses, current relevance, and first-time patterns.
The lesson guide also explains useful vocabulary and expressions from the episode: embargo, press note, news release, committee, panel, to reach a decision, present perfect, past simple, public mood, momentum, and other useful advanced-English phrases.
On the episode page you will find the study guide, comprehension questions, answers, notes on usage, the slow dialogue, the natural-speed dialogue, and the full transcript.
item.content:encoded:
Lesson guide and full transcript: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/003-the-embargoed-award

In this episode of Vox English, we work with the past simple and the present perfect in advanced public-communication English.

The episode is called "The Embargoed Award". Priya and Elliot prepare university communication around an award result. The scene turns embargoes, news releases, timing, and professional reputation into a lesson in how English frames completed facts and current relevance.

This lesson helps you use past simple and present perfect with news-report openings, finished time expressions, future time clauses, since clauses, current relevance, and first-time patterns.

The lesson guide also explains useful vocabulary and expressions from the episode: embargo, press note, news release, committee, panel, to reach a decision, present perfect, past simple, public mood, momentum, and other useful advanced-English phrases.

On the episode page you will find the study guide, comprehension questions, answers, notes on usage, the slow dialogue, the natural-speed dialogue, and the full transcript.
